article_meta.title: ...By Natalie VenegasWeekend ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberProtests have erupted across the country ove

reasoning: Mostly neutral summary that includes multiple perspectives and avoids most charged framing. Minor adoption of source wording such as “crackdown” and “protests have erupted,” and the politically loaded term “undocumented immigrants,” but no systematic inheritance of sensational or partisan language.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses a few interpretive terms (“crackdown,” “erupted”) but otherwise sticks to factual verbs and attributions.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key claims are attributed to polls, officials, or local reporting; numbers and arrests include sources.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Avoids asserting evaluative judgments; notes concerns without endorsing them.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Slightly heightened tone with “erupted/crackdown,” but no amplification beyond article.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers protests and opposition along with polling support and White House stance; slight emphasis on protests consistent with article focus.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures major perspectives (administration, advocates, protesters, law enforcement, polling); omits detailed quotes from specific politicians but acknowledges their category.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal subjective descriptors; mostly descriptive language.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Choice of “undocumented immigrants” and “crackdown” carries political connotations.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balances concerns with polling support and official statements.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and grassroots sources similarly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source but retains a couple of loaded terms.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes the main viewpoints present, proportionally focused on protests as in the source.
